nav.title: Atrex-Bench
hero.title: Atrex-Bench
hero.subtitle: End-to-End Operator Generation Benchmark for Multiple DSLs
hero.description: Production-trace driven benchmark for evaluating "agent platform + model" stacks on GPU kernel generation. Given a PyTorch reference, measure whether the system can produce a kernel that compiles, runs correctly, and approaches the achievable hardware peak.
overview.title: Why Atrex-Bench?
overview.intro: Unlike existing operator benchmarks that rely on open-source datasets with toy-sized shapes, Atrex-Bench sources its operators and input distributions directly from production cluster traces — ensuring benchmark results map to real deployment scenarios.
overview.feature1.title: Production-Trace Driven
overview.feature1.desc: Operators and shapes extracted from real cluster traces (vLLM, SGLang, AITER, RTP-LLM), reflecting actual production workloads rather than synthetic or academic examples.
overview.feature2.title: Executable PyTorch References
overview.feature2.desc: Each operator ships a complete PyTorch reference implementation. No ground-truth kernel code — evaluation is purely through compilation, correctness, and profiling.
overview.feature3.title: Roofline-Based Evaluation
overview.feature3.desc: Performance measured against hardware roofline upper bound (W/Q/P_peak/B_peak), providing absolute hardware utilization metrics rather than relative speedup.
overview.compare.title: How We Differ
pipeline.title: Evaluation Pipeline
pipeline.intro: A three-stage evaluation pipeline with isolated generation and evaluation environments. The agent generates code, which is then evaluated in a sandboxed GPU environment.
pipeline.stage0.title: Stage 0: Compilation
pipeline.stage0.desc: Import the candidate module and verify no compilation errors. Triton JIT compilation is triggered on first kernel launch.
pipeline.stage1.title: Stage 1: Correctness
pipeline.stage1.desc: Compare candidate output against PyTorch reference using torch.allclose with configurable tolerances (atol=1e-2, rtol=5e-2).
pipeline.stage2.title: Stage 2: Performance
pipeline.stage2.desc: Benchmark the candidate kernel with warmup + 100 iterations, measuring median end-to-end time against the roofline Speed-of-Light lower bound.
pipeline.roofline.title: Roofline Model
pipeline.metrics.title: Core Metrics
pipeline.metrics.compile.desc: Fraction of operators that compile successfully
pipeline.metrics.correct.desc: Fraction that produce numerically correct output
pipeline.metrics.hwutil.desc: Median hardware utilization vs roofline upper bound
operators.title: Operator Catalog
operators.intro: 30 production-grade operators extracted from real cluster traces, covering attention, MoE, normalization, quantization, and more.
operators.table.name: Operator
operators.chart.title: Operator Importance Distribution
operators.chart.subtitle: Importance score reflects trace-derived GPU time share across production workloads
data.page.title: Benchmark Data
quickstart.title: Quick Start
quickstart.intro: Get up and running with Atrex-Bench in minutes using Docker and the built-in CLI tools.
quickstart.docker.title: Run with Docker
quickstart.docker.desc: The ROCm runtime image bundles PyTorch, AITER, FlyDSL, and evaluation tools.
quickstart.eval.title: Run Evaluation
quickstart.eval.desc: Evaluate a candidate kernel against a reference operator.
quickstart.gen.title: Generate Kernel
quickstart.gen.desc: Use an LLM CLI to generate a candidate kernel from a reference.
dataformat.title: Data Format
dataformat.intro: Each benchmark sample is an operator directory with five files following a strict contract.
